title: T-038 — tenant_migrations table migration (canary upgrade orchestration baseline)
prompt:
# T-038 — tenant_migrations table migration (canary upgrade orchestration baseline)

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §8 (Upgrade orchestration)
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Create the tenant_migrations Drizzle table that records which tenant is on which version of each component, used by canary upgrade orchestration (5-10% canary → progressive). The table is the data substrate for §8 'Tenant migrations table: (tenant_id, component, current_version, target_version, last_run_at)'.

## Implementation contract
(1) Add packages/db/src/schema/tenant-migrations.ts with Drizzle table tenantMigrations { tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REF organizations(id), component TEXT NOT NULL, current_version TEXT NOT NULL, target_version TEXT, cohort TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'general' CHECK IN ('canary','early','general'), last_run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'idle' CHECK IN ('idle','pending','running','succeeded','failed','rolled_back'), failure_reason TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, component) }. (2) Generate migration file packages/db/migrations/0008_tenant_migrations.sql with the table + index on (cohort, status). (3) Export the schema from packages/db/src/schema/index.ts. (4) Add Vitest unit asserting Drizzle infers tenant_id as UUID and cohort enum CHECK is present.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/src/schema/tenant-migrations.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/migrations/0008_tenant_migrations.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/__tests__/tenant-migrations.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs from empty, when 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db migrate' completes, then table tenant_migrations exists with all 8 columns (tenant_id, component, current_version, target_version, cohort, last_run_at, status, failure_reason) and PK = (tenant_id, component)
2. Given a row INSERT with cohort='moonshot' (not in CHECK list), when SQL runs, then Postgres rejects the row with a CHECK constraint violation
3. Given a row exists with status='running', when a second INSERT for the same (tenant_id, component) is attempted, then the PK constraint rejects it (no duplicate orchestration)
4. Given the Drizzle schema is imported, when 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db typecheck' runs, then tenant_id is inferred as string (UUID) and cohort union literal includes only 'canary' | 'early' | 'general'

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/src/__tests__/tenant-migrations.test.ts covering all four ACs before the schema file exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test'

## Risk red lines
- Do not add an upgrade orchestration Server Action in this task (covered by T-039)
- Do not allow status updates to skip the audit_events emit — the API task wires that up
- Do not partition this table — single global table is correct per §8 (small cardinality)

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-006

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Drizzle table tenant_migrations recording per-tenant component versions + cohort + status for canary-first upgrade rollouts.
pipeline_inputs.details: PRD §8 names this table verbatim: '(tenant_id, component, current_version, target_version, last_run_at)'. We extend it with cohort (canary/early/general) + status enum + failure_reason because §8 also says canary 5-10% → monitor 15-30min → progressive — the orchestrator needs both cohort segmentation and run state.
out_of_scope: Do not modify files outside the scope_files list
Do not introduce business logic from later module PRDs (01-NPD, 02-SETTINGS, etc.)
